commit.message:
fix: keep restart.signal alive during restart to prevent premature banner dismissal

The launcher was deleting restart.signal immediately when picked up, but
restart() can take minutes (draining sessions, building, graceful shutdown).
The server's watchdog saw the file disappear and cleared the restart banner
after 90s — long before the restart actually happened.

Now the signal file is only deleted in the finally block after restart()
completes or fails. On build-failure rollback, the surviving server's
banner is dismissed immediately via POST /api/restart-clear.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pass ServeHeaderOptions by value instead of pointer, fine tune httplib tests (#36982)

Pass `ServeHeaderOptions` by value instead of pointer across all call
sites — no nil-check semantics are needed and the struct is small enough
that copying is fine.

## Changes

- **`services/context/base.go`**: `SetServeHeaders` and `ServeContent`
accept `ServeHeaderOptions` (value, not pointer); internal unsafe
pointer cast replaced with a clean type conversion
- **`routers/api/packages/helper/helper.go`**: `ServePackageFile`
variadic changed from `...*context.ServeHeaderOptions` to
`...context.ServeHeaderOptions`; internal variable is now a value type
- **All call sites** (13 files): `&context.ServeHeaderOptions{...}` →
`context.ServeHeaderOptions{...}`

Before/after at the definition level:
```go
// Before
func (b *Base) SetServeHeaders(opt *ServeHeaderOptions) { ... }
func (b *Base) ServeContent(r io.ReadSeeker, opts *ServeHeaderOptions) { ... }
func ServePackageFile(..., forceOpts ...*context.ServeHeaderOptions) { ... }

// After
func (b *Base) SetServeHeaders(opts ServeHeaderOptions) { ... }
func (b *Base) ServeContent(r io.ReadSeeker, opts ServeHeaderOptions) { ... }
func ServePackageFile(..., forceOpts ...context.ServeHeaderOptions) { ... }
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: wxiaoguang <2114189+wxiaoguang@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: wxiaoguang <wxiaoguang@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Improve staging deploy: auto-rebase before merge, non-destructive conflict recovery

- staging_deploy now rebases staging branch onto latest production HEAD before merging,
  handling the common "production moved forward" case automatically
- Commit step is idempotent: skipped on retry when no uncommitted changes exist
- On rebase conflict, worktree is preserved with step-by-step resolution guidance
  instead of the old "cleanup + init from scratch" approach
- Updated tool descriptions: staging_cleanup clarifies it's for abandoning work only
- Updated STAGING_INSTRUCTIONS system prompt with conflict recovery guidance

commit.message:
fix: restore staged backends for surviving previews after deploy restart

Previously, when staging preview B was deployed and the server restarted,
any other active preview A would lose its staged backend (API router +
context). The frontend would still load but all API calls would fail.

Now pruneOrphanedWorktrees() is async and restores staged backends for
surviving previews on startup by re-running seedStagingData →
createStagingContext → initialize → register router. If restoration fails,
the preview is fully cleaned up instead of left half-broken.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Renamed .yml → .yml.disabled. Project uses Cat CI system,
not GitHub Actions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

capavisor: doorbell fast-path for child EPT violations, virtio-console boot

Child domain EPT_VIOLATION exits were falling through to the catch-all
forward_child_exit path, bypassing the doorbell handler entirely. This
caused every virtio queue kick to take the slow MMIO emulation path
through CHV, triggering 'Unexpected write to notification BAR' errors
and soft lockups.

Fix: add EXIT_REASON_EPT_VIOLATION as an explicit match arm in the
child exit handler, checking handle_ept_doorbell() before forwarding.
Also simplify the dom0 EPT doorbell check to use if-let.

run-dom1.sh changes:
- Default to 1 vCPU (CHV_CPUS=1) for single-VP debugging
- Add -v flag for CHV info-level logging
- Console already switched to virtio (console=hvc0) in prior commit

With this fix, dom1 boots to multi-user.target (login prompt) with
zero panics or triple faults. Doorbell notifications flow correctly
through the capavisor fast-path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add OWL ontology tutorial page with embedded live demos

New files:
- packages/web/public/owl-tutorial.html
  Full step-by-step tutorial: start from template → add ontology
  header → place classes → build hierarchy → add instances →
  add object properties → run auto-layout → export to Turtle.
  Each section has prose instructions and an embedded live BoxesEditor
  iframe pre-loaded with that stage of the Animals ontology.
  Includes a highlighted Turtle code block for the export step,
  a sticky TOC with IntersectionObserver active-section tracking,
  and a responsive dark-theme layout.

- packages/web/public/demo.html + demo.js
  Thin iframe-friendly BoxesEditor wrapper.
  Params: ?graph=<url> to load a .boxes file, ?tab=<id> to activate
  a sidebar tab, ?sidebar=0 to hide the sidebar for canvas-only views.

- packages/web/public/demos/owl-{1..7}-*.boxes
  Seven incremental demo graphs for the tutorial iframes, each a
  valid .boxes file (OWL palette + stylesheet + Animals ontology
  elements at preset positions):
    1-empty      — empty canvas, palette visible
    2-ontology   — owl:Ontology header node
    3-classes    — 7 owl:Class nodes, no edges yet
    4-hierarchy  — subClassOf edges connected
    5-instances  — individuals typed via rdf:type
    6-properties — owl:ObjectProperty + Person class
    7-complete   — full graph ready for layout

Modified:
- packages/core/src/boxes-editor.js
  Add public setActiveTab(tabId) method so demo.js can activate
  a specific sidebar tab programmatically.

- packages/web/public/index.html
  Add 'Building an OWL Ontology' tutorial link on welcome screen.

- packages/web/vite.config.js
  Add demo.html + owl-tutorial.html as Rollup entry points.
  Add copyDemoFiles() plugin to copy demos/*.boxes → dist/demos/
  during production build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add odt-convert to README skill catalog

* feat: Add OpenRouter as primary provider with task-specific model routing

Configure OpenRouter as the primary LLM provider with intelligent
model selection per task type:

- code_generation → Qwen3 Coder (purpose-built for code, 262K ctx)
- planning → DeepSeek Chat V3.1 (strong reasoning)
- analysis → Gemini 2.5 Flash (1M ctx for large codebases)
- critique/quality → DeepSeek R1 (deep reasoning)
- fast/ingest/verification → Gemini 2.0 Flash (cheapest capable)
- fallback → Llama 3.3 70B (free, rate-limit safety net)

Changes:
- settings.json: primary_provider=openrouter, 11 task routes
- model_adapter.py: OpenRouter first in respond() fallback chain,
  respond_for_role() routes to task-specific models via OpenRouter,
  call_openrouter() uses model_routing config with auto-fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename mnemonic → story and fix exported-row column mapping

- Rename 'mnemonic' field to 'story' across model, config, parser,
  CLI, templates, CSS, and all tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: rename all test methods to Subject_Result_WhenCondition

WhenCondition is omitted when self-evident from Subject or Result.

Tests renamed (40 total across 5 files):

ConcurrencyTests (2)
  EveryTrigger_ConcurrentCalls_*  → EveryTrigger_*_WhenCalledConcurrently
  OnceTrigger_ConcurrentCalls_*   → OnceTrigger_*_WhenCalledConcurrently

LoopTests (10)
  RunAsync_Once_*                 → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingOnceTrigger
  RunAsync_After_*                → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingAfterTrigger
  RunAsync_Every_*                → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingEveryTrigger
  RunAsync_Cancellation*          → RunAsync_*_WhenCancelled*
  RunAsync_ErrorPolicy*           → RunAsync_*_WhenErrorPolicy*
  RunAsync_CustomOnError_*        → RunAsync_*_WhenCustomErrorHandler*
  RunAsync_WhenAny_*              → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingWhenAnyTrigger

TriggerTests (16) — WhenCondition dropped where obvious:
  Once_FiresOnce                  → Once_ReturnsNullOnSecondCall
  Once_FiresImmediately           → Once_ReturnsTimeCloseToNow
  After_FiresOnceAfterDelay       → After_SchedulesAfterDelayAndExhausts
  Every_FiresRepeatedly           → Every_SchedulesOnFixedInterval
  WhenAny_PicksEarliest           → WhenAny_ReturnsEarliestTime
  WhenAll_PicksLatest             → WhenAll_ReturnsLatestTime
  (others retain WhenCondition to disambiguate)

LoggingMiddlewareTests (3), RetryMiddlewareTests (6),
MiddlewarePipelineTests (3) — same rules applied.

Docs: CONTRIBUTING.md — new Test naming section with table, examples,
      and explicit guidance on when WhenCondition can be omitted.
      AGENTS.md — one-liner in Conventions pointing at CONTRIBUTING.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: cli
repository.description: The CLI for AI Agents and human

commit.message:
Expand guided tour from 9 to 14 steps

New steps cover features not previously in the tour:
- Undo & Redo (↩/↪ buttons and Ctrl+Z/Y shortcuts)
- Stylesheet tab — editing Cytoscape.js CSS rules inline
- Palette editing — adding/editing/deleting node and edge types
- Import & Export — RDF/Turtle, JSON-LD, RDF/XML menus
- Templates & Reuse — .boxes files as reusable templates, Save As workflow

Updated existing steps:
- Welcome is now generic (not OWL-specific)
- Canvas step adds Fit (F), Move, and trackpad pinch-zoom
- Palette step clarifies Esc to deactivate
- Properties step notes live updates
- Layout step adds more algorithms (Concentric, Grid, Circle, Random)
- Save step expanded to cover Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S), Open, and New
- Final step uses generic graph workflow instead of OWL-specific

Bumped TOUR_DONE_KEY to 'boxes-tour-v2-done' so returning users
who completed v1 see the expanded tour automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  description, context)

- New 'Using a .boxes file as a template' section with loadTemplateFromUrl
- Add paletteChanged event to events table
- Add 'template' prop to Vue and React props tables

- Add onPaletteChanged to React props table
- Update Vue exposed ref methods to include palette mutation methods
- Add template/palette architecture note

packages/web/README.md:

- Add 'Working with Palettes' section with full UI workflow

- Add palette API examples

WEB_SERVER.md:

- Minor cleanup

commit.message:
chore: add icon.png and wire PackageIcon for NuGet packages

Converts icon.svg to a 128x128 PNG (Inkscape export) — NuGet.org requires
a raster image for package icons; SVG is not supported.

src/Directory.Build.props: adds PackageIcon=icon.png and bundles icon.png
into every package alongside README.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add clean target to Makefile

Runs dotnet clean and removes artifacts/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: lock AssemblyVersion to major, FileVersion to full numeric version

Adds src/Directory.Build.targets (imported after SDK targets, so $(Version)
is fully resolved) with two overrides for all library projects:

  AssemblyVersion = major.0.0.0
    Locked to the major version so consumers compiled against v1.0 don't
    need to recompile for v1.1/v1.2 (binding-redirect / strong-name safety).

  FileVersion = major.minor.patch.0
    Pre-release label stripped (regex match on '^\d+\.\d+\.\d+') because
    Windows file-version fields only accept numeric components.

  InformationalVersion = x.y.z[-pre]+<commit-sha>
    Left to the SDK default — carries the full SemVer label plus source hash.

Verified against three cases:
  -p:Version=1.2.3      → AV=1.0.0.0  FV=1.2.3.0   IV=1.2.3+sha
  -p:Version=2.0.0-beta.1 → AV=2.0.0.0  FV=2.0.0.0   IV=2.0.0-beta.1+sha
  default (0.1.0)       → AV=0.0.0.0  FV=0.1.0.0   IV=0.1.0+sha

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add NuGet packaging and publish pipeline

Directory.Build.props (root)
- Centralises TargetFramework, Nullable, ImplicitUsings for all projects
- Sets IsPackable=false and GenerateDocumentationFile=false as repo defaults

src/Directory.Build.props
- Overrides IsPackable=true, GenerateDocumentationFile=true for library projects
- Enables IncludeSymbols + SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg
- Shared NuGet metadata: Authors, PackageTags, RepositoryUrl, PackageReadmeFile
- Bundles README.md into every package; VersionPrefix=0.1.0

csproj changes
- src/Looop: add PackageId=KatzuoOgust.Looop, Description; remove properties now
  in Directory.Build.props
- src/Looop.AspNetCore: same, PackageId=KatzuoOgust.Looop.AspNetCore
- tests/Looop.Tests: remove IsPackable=false (root default), TargetFramework,
  Nullable, ImplicitUsings
- examples/*: remove TargetFramework, Nullable, ImplicitUsings

XML doc fixes (surfaced by GenerateDocumentationFile=true)
- ITrigger.NextAsync: add param/return comments
- IJobMiddleware: fix broken <paramref> on delegate, add InvokeAsync comment
- MiddlewareAwareJob: add constructor summary

Makefile: add pack and push targets
CI: add Pack (verify) step after tests
publish.yml: new workflow triggered on v*.*.* tags;
  strips leading 'v' for dotnet pack -p:Version, pushes .nupkg + .snupkg

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add EVDI diagnostic screenshot to main README
- Improve overall structure with clear sections and subsections
- Add component overview table for clarity
- Enhance Getting Started guide with step-by-step instructions
- Add comprehensive command reference table
- Reorganize Trainer section with configuration table
- Add capture backends comparison table

This makes the README more accessible to new users and provides better navigation for different skill levels.

commit.message:
Fix schedule SSE events not emitting from agent tool handlers

Add globalBus.emit({ type: "schedule:changed" }) to schedule_create,
schedule_update, and schedule_delete tool handlers in session-manager.ts,
matching the API route behavior. Also emit schedule:changed from
scheduler.ts after a schedule runs so runCount/lastRunAt/nextRunAt
updates push to the UI instantly.

commit.message:
chore: add project icon (icon.svg)

Three concentric circular arrows on a dark background, one per 'o' in
'Looop'. Each arc spans 300° clockwise with an arrowhead at 10 o'clock;
colors graduate sky-blue → indigo → violet from outer to inner ring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

style: enforce beginning-of-line operator placement when wrapping

Adds dotnet_style_operator_placement_when_wrapping = beginning_of_line
so &&, ||, and => lead the continuation line when an expression wraps.
Codebase already conforms; no source files changed by dotnet format.

Simplifies CONTRIBUTING code style note to a single general statement
pointing at .editorconfig as the single source of truth.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add theme-aware left border on unread task rows

Unread tasks in the expanded TaskRail and TaskList now show a
border-l-2 using border-text-primary (white in dark mode, black in
light mode) for a Discord-style unread indicator. Coexists with the
existing active-selection border and green dot/bold title indicators.

commit.message:
fix(payroll): role-aware compound key allows idea-originator + implementer payouts to same wallet (#196)

* Initial plan

* fix: payroll queue blocking error — remove duplicate on-chain keys, fix fractional amounts

Root cause: issues #187, #189, #192 each had @TheJollyLaMa listed as both
'implementer' AND 'idea-originator' with the same wallet address. Both entries
produced identical entryKey() compound keys, causing the second entry to revert
with 'Treasury: issue already paid' during batchPayContributors.

Changes:
- payroll-queue.json: remove 3 duplicate TheJollyLaMa 'implementer' entries
  (#187, #189, #192); idea-originator entries are kept intact
- contributor-accounts.json: decrease TheJollyLaMa bnutPending by 2.4 BNUT
  (3 × 0.8 removed) → 43.2
- js/app.js: replace parseInt → parseFloat (with positive-number guard) in both
  per-row and batch payout paths so fractional bounties (0.8, 0.4 BNUT) are
  sent at the correct value instead of being floored to 0 → rounded up to 1
- scripts/validatePayrollQueue.js: add check 5b — detects duplicate on-chain
  compound keys (issueRef:contributorAddress) that would cause a contract revert

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TheJollyLaMa/BigNuten_Vanilla/sessions/258e1d9c-77b3-4f6d-ba27-3dbab48f9566

* fix(payroll): role-aware compound key — idea-originator + implementer coexist for same wallet

When a contributor both originates an idea (20% credit) and implements the
solution (80%), two independent queue entries exist for the same wallet+issue.
The old entryKey() used `issueRef:wallet` (no role), so both entries produced
an identical on-chain key — the contract reverted on the second with
"Treasury: issue already paid".

Fix: include role in compound key when present:
  issueRef:wallet:implementer   — 80% implementation share
  issueRef:wallet:idea-originator — 20% idea-credit share
  issueRef:wallet               — legacy entries (no role, unchanged)

Changes:
- js/app.js entryKey(): append :role suffix when p.role is set
- js/app.js per-row Send button: fix p.issueRef → entryKey(p) for the
  contract call (was using raw ref instead of compound key)
- js/treasury.js getContributorPaidEvents: update strip regex to also
  remove :role suffix for display
- payroll-queue.json: restore 3 TheJollyLaMa implementer entries for
  #187/#189/#192 (wrongly removed in previous PR — they are legitimate pay)
- contributor-accounts.json: restore TheJollyLaMa bnutPending 43.2→45.6
- scripts/validatePayrollQueue.js onChainKey(): include role in key
- bounty-bot.yml (Job1 + Job2): role-aware implementer deduplication so
  a contributor who is also idea-originator is not skipped as implementer

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TheJollyLaMa/BigNuten_Vanilla/sessions/f3350818-fa9a-49c6-9a46-ea8e2d85968e

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
